summary: Iteration 4 - Complete testing of Jurisdiction Finder module. All features working: 306 stations via API, map click finds nearest station using Haversine formula, search functionality, Zero FIR letter generation. Backend: 13/13 tests passed. Frontend: All features working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: /api/jurisdiction/stations returns 306 stations
Backend: Station data has required fields (name, district, latitude, longitude, phone)
Backend: Stations cover 30+ Telangana districts (Hyderabad, Cyberabad, Warangal, etc.)
Backend: /api/jurisdiction/find returns nearest station for Gachibowli coords
Backend: Find API returns 10 nearby stations sorted by distance
Backend: Haversine distance calculation accurate (~0km for exact station coords)
Backend: Find station in Warangal area works
Backend: Find station in rural southern Telangana works
Backend: Search station by name (Madhapur) works
Backend: Search stations by district (Cyberabad - 40+ stations) works
Backend: Station data complete for Zero FIR generation
Backend: /api/jurisdiction/stations requires JWT authentication (401 without token)
Backend: /api/jurisdiction/find requires JWT authentication (401 without token)
Frontend: Login with OFF12345/test123 credentials works
Frontend: Jurisdiction Finder page loads showing '306 stations loaded'
Frontend: Map displays with Leaflet/OpenStreetMap tiles
Frontend: Station markers (cyan dots) visible on map across Telangana
Frontend: Map click on empty area triggers findNearest API
Frontend: Nearest station details panel shows station name, district, distance, phone, coordinates
Frontend: Nearby Stations list shows 6 nearby stations with distances
Frontend: Toast notification shows nearest station name and distance
Frontend: Search input for station/district works
Frontend: Search for 'Madhapur' finds Madhapur PS in Cyberabad
Frontend: Map zooms to searched station location
Frontend: Zero FIR Transfer Letter button appears after finding station
Frontend: Zero FIR form opens with all fields (Station Name, Complainant Name, Phone, Address, Date, Location, Brief Facts)
Frontend: Zero FIR form has explanatory note about transfer process
Frontend: Download Zero FIR Transfer Letter PDF button works
Frontend: Success toast shows 'Zero FIR Transfer Letter generated!'
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_jurisdiction_finder.py - Created comprehensive test suite for Jurisdiction Finder
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test user and 306 stations from JSON file
context_for_next_testing_agent: Jurisdiction Finder module is fully functional. Backend APIs working with 306 police stations. Frontend correctly loads stations, displays map with markers, handles map clicks for nearest station lookup using Haversine formula, search functionality works, and Zero FIR PDF generation is operational. All issues from iteration 3 have been resolved.

mocked_apis.note: All Jurisdiction Finder APIs are real implementations using local JSON data file
